decisions.briefing: Markets drifted slightly positive today with NASDAQ +0.1% and S&P 500 +0.2% — a quiet, directionless session. All six current positions remain healthy with no stop-loss or profit-target triggers. Cash sits at $444.94, just below the $500 threshold that would trigger new buys, so we hold tight and let existing positions run. NESR and SOFI remain top watchlist candidates for when cash is replenished via profit-taking.

market_education.summary_en: Today's session was a textbook 'consolidation day' — both the S&P 500 and NASDAQ posted fractional gains as investors digested recent rallies without committing to a new directional move. With no major catalysts or economic releases driving conviction, volume was light and price action stayed range-bound. This type of subdued trading often follows a strong run-up period as the market 'catches its breath' before the next leg higher or lower.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場呈典型的整理格局，標普500及納斯達克均小幅收高，投資者在消化近期升幅後未有明顯方向性操作。由於缺乏重要催化劑或經濟數據提振信心，市場成交量偏淡，股價走勢橫行收窄。這種低波動的交易日通常出現於一段強勁升市之後，市場正在「喘息整固」，等待下一個明確方向。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance penalty a portfolio suffers when it holds uninvested cash instead of putting money to work in the market. While cash provides safety and flexibility ('dry powder'), it earns little and misses out on market gains — today our $444 in cash contributes nothing to returns while our invested positions compound.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合因持有未投資的現金而承受的回報損失。雖然現金提供了安全性與靈活性（即所謂的「備用子彈」），但它幾乎不產生收益，也無法分享市場升幅。今日我們持有的444美元現金對整體回報毫無貢獻，而已投資的部位則持續複利增長。
